stimulus: “The Mirror” is a 1908 poem by Cordelia Hayes. In the poem, Hayes suggests that her readers, whom she addresses directly, have not fully recognized their own strength, writing, ______
stem: Which quotation from “The Mirror” most effectively illustrates the claim?

answerOptions.content: “The river runs deep and wide before you, / Sing now to its waters as you pass / Beside its banks at the close of day.”
“You have not known your own measure, you have stood blind / before the glass, / Your hand has been a stranger to its own shape and use.”
“I will praise your name in every song I make, / I shall write nothing but of you and your kind.”
“The fields will yield their grain at season’s end, / And the hills will hold the snow in winter still.”

rationale:
Choice B is correct because the lines directly tell the reader “You have not known your own measure” and stood “blind / before the glass,” clearly conveying that the reader has not fully recognized themselves or their own strength.

Choice A is incorrect: it describes a river, not the reader’s self-knowledge. Choice C is incorrect: it focuses on the speaker’s praise, not the reader’s lack of self-recognition. Choice D is incorrect: it describes fields and hills, not the reader.
